info.title: MCPForge API
info.description: Convert OpenAPI specs into AI-optimized MCP servers

post.description:
Register a new user account.

Raises:
    409: Email already registered.
    422: Password appears in HIBP.

Authenticate and log in.

Raises:
    401: Invalid credentials.
    423: Account locked (too many failed attempts).

Refresh the access token using a refresh token from cookie.

Exempt from CSRF (the cookie carries the credential; the body is empty).
Implements refresh token rotation with replay detection: reusing an
already-used jti revokes the user's entire token family.

Log out: revoke all refresh tokens + clear auth cookies.

Auth is optional: if the request is authenticated, we revoke the
user's token family; if not, we just clear the cookies. This
matches the legacy behavior (logout always returns 200) and the
new hardening (authed requests get a full token-family revoke).

schema.title: Response Logout Api V1 Auth Logout Post

get.description: Get the currently authenticated user's profile.

post.description:
Request a password reset email.

Always returns 200 with the same message, regardless of whether the
email exists in the system (prevents user enumeration).  Rate-limited
to 3 requests per email per hour.

Reset a password using a token from the email.

On success, all existing sessions are invalidated and the user must
log in again.

Raises:
    422: Token invalid/expired/used, or the password is in HIBP.

Verify the authenticated user's email using a verification token.

The token was sent via email during registration (or via
``/resend-verification``).  Must be authenticated.

Raises:
    422: Token invalid or expired.

Resend the email-verification email.

Must be authenticated.  Generates a fresh token and sends a new email.
Does NOT invalidate any previously sent tokens (all remain valid until
their 24-hour expiry or until the user verifies).

get.description:
Start GitHub OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow.

Generates a CSRF-protecting state token, stores it in Redis, and
redirects the user to GitHub's authorization page.

Returns:
    302 redirect to ``https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize?...``.

Raises:
    503: If GitHub OAuth is not configured (``GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID``
        is empty).

Handle the OAuth callback from GitHub.

Verifies the CSRF state parameter, exchanges the authorization code
for an access token, fetches the user's GitHub profile and verified
email, and upserts the user in the database.

On success, sets auth cookies and CSRF cookie, then redirects to
``FRONTEND_URL?oauth=success``. On failure, redirects with
``?oauth=error=<code>``.

All errors result in a redirect (never raw JSON) because the user's
browser is in the middle of the OAuth redirect chain — showing JSON
would be a poor UX. The frontend parses the ``oauth`` query param
and displays appropriate feedback.

Raises:
    503: If GitHub OAuth is not configured.
Health check endpoint for the API service.
List all MCP servers for the current user.

schema.title: Response List Servers Api V1 Servers Get

post.description: Create a new MCP server.

get.description: Get a specific MCP server by ID.

post.description:
Duplicate an existing MCP server.

Creates a new server with the same configuration (tools_config)
but resets version, stats, and status. Credentials are NOT copied.
The new server starts in ``building`` status.

get.description: List version history for a server. Pending Phase 7.

schema.title: Response List Versions Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Versions Get

post.description: Roll back to a previous server version. Pending Phase 7.

schema.title: Response Rollback Server Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Rollback Post

post.description:
Fetch an OpenAPI spec from a URL, parse, validate, and return extracted tools.

The spec is downloaded from the provided URL, validated against OpenAPI 3.0+,
stored in Cloudflare R2, and analyzed for MCP tool definitions.

Upload an OpenAPI spec file (multipart, JSON or YAML, ≤5MB).

The uploaded file is parsed, validated against OpenAPI 3.0+, stored in
Cloudflare R2, and analyzed for MCP tool definitions.

get.description:
Get the parsed list of MCP tools extracted from a spec.

Fetches the spec content from Cloudflare R2, re-parses it, and extracts
tool definitions. Verifies the requesting user owns the spec.

post.description:
Select tools from a spec and create a new MCP server.

Re-parses the spec from R2, applies the user's tool selection and
customizations, generates the MCP tools configuration, and creates
a new MCPServer with that configuration.

get.description:
Get a spec's metadata.

Returns metadata about the fetched/uploaded OpenAPI spec including
title, version, endpoint count, and fetch status.

delete.description:
Delete a spec and its stored content.

Deletes the spec content from Cloudflare R2 (best-effort — if the R2
object is missing, we log a warning and continue) and removes the
metadata row from the database.

get.description: List all tools for a server.

patch.description: Update a single tool (description, enabled, rename).

schema.title: Response Update Tool Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Tools  Tool Name  Patch

post.description: Re-run AI enhancement on the server's tools.

get.description: List all credentials for a server (never returns values).

post.description: Add a credential for a server (encrypted at rest).
Dry-run a request using the stored credential.
Start a build — runs the AI enhancement pipeline via ServerBuilder.

get.description: SSE stream of build events — real-time AI enhancement progress.

post.description: Accept AI-proposed tool description enhancements.

schema.title: Response Accept Ai Enhancements Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Tools Accept Post

post.description: Deploy the server (triggers security scan first).

schema.title: Response Deploy Server Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Deploy Post

post.description: Trigger a security scan (runs as a background Celery task).

get.description: Get the most recent security scan result. Returns ``null`` if no scan has been run yet.

schema.title: Response Get Latest Scan Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Security Latest Get

get.description: List security scan history for a server (paginated).

post.description: Acknowledge a security finding (suppresses in future scans).

delete.description: Remove an acknowledgment for a finding.

get.description: Export the latest scan as a full security report.
List all acknowledgments for a server.

schema.title: Response List Acknowledgments Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Security Acknowledgments Get

get.description: GET /analytics — top-line numbers for a range. Pending F6.

schema.title: Response Analytics Overview Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Analytics Get

get.description: Per-tool call counts, error rates, selection rates. Pending F6.

schema.title: Response Tool Breakdown Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Analytics Tools Get

get.description: Paginated error log (sanitized, no parameter values). Pending F6.

schema.title: Response Error Log Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Analytics Errors Get

get.description: Breakdown of calls by client (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc). Pending F6.

schema.title: Response Client Breakdown Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Analytics Clients Get

get.description: Time-series of calls. Pending F6.

schema.title: Response Timeseries Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Analytics Timeseries Get

get.description: CSV export of all tool calls. Pending F6.

schema.title: Response Export Csv Api V1 Servers  Server Id  Analytics Export Csv Get

get.description: Get the current user's team.

post.description: Create a new team for the current user.
Invite a user by email. Admin only.
Accept a team invitation by token.

get.description: List all team members.

schema.title: Response List Members Api V1 Team Members Get

patch.description: Update a member's role. Admin only.

delete.description: Remove a team member. Admin only, or self-removal.

get.description: List audit log entries. Admin only.
List all API keys for the current user.

post.description: Create a new API key. The plaintext key is returned ONCE.

delete.description: Revoke an API key. Verifies ownership first.

get.description: List available plans (free, pro, team). No authentication required.

post.description:
Create a Stripe Checkout session for subscribing to a plan.

If the user does not yet have a Stripe customer ID, one is created
first.
Create a Stripe Customer Portal session for managing the subscription.

Receive Stripe webhook events.

Signature-verified and idempotent via 7-day Redis TTL on event IDs.
No authentication required.

schema.title: Response Stripe Webhook Api V1 Billing Webhook Post

get.description:
Get the current user's active subscription.

Raises 404 if no active subscription is found.
List invoices for the current user's subscription (paginated).

Get connection details for the gateway.

Returns URLs and copy-ready config snippets for Claude Desktop and
Cursor so the user can plug their MCP server into any MCP host.

post.description:
Dry-run a connectivity check against the upstream server.

Attempts an HTTP GET to the server's ``base_url`` to verify the
upstream is reachable and measure round-trip latency. This is a
lightweight connectivity test — the full MCP ``tools/list``
handshake requires the gateway pipeline (Phase 2).

Pause a server — stop accepting requests but retain config.

The paused server will appear as ``paused`` in the dashboard and
the gateway will reject incoming MCP requests until the server is
resumed.

Resume a paused server — re-enable MCP requests.

Restores the server to ``active`` status so the gateway once again
accepts incoming MCP requests.

get.description: Health check for a specific MCP server (public).

schema.title: Response Mcp Server Health Mcp V1  Slug  Health Get

get.description: SSE transport endpoint for MCP protocol (auth required).

post.description: Message endpoint for SSE transport (auth required).

get.description:
GET on the StreamableHTTP endpoint — returns an SSE heartbeat stream.

Per the MCP StreamableHTTP spec, the GET endpoint is used to open a
long-lived connection for server-initiated messages (heartbeats).

post.description:
StreamableHTTP transport endpoint for MCP protocol (auth required).

Accepts JSON-RPC 2.0 requests and returns JSON-RPC responses.
Session tracking uses the ``MCP-Session-Id`` request/response header.

get.description:
Root health check.

Pings Postgres, Redis, and (best-effort) the Celery worker pool. A
failure in any dependency is reported as `"unavailable"`/`"down"` in
the response; the endpoint itself returns 200 to keep load balancers
happy (use 503-style probing for actual readiness).

tool_names.title: Tool Names
tool_names.description: If None, enhances all tools. Otherwise, only the named ones.

force.title: Force
force.description: If True, re-run even on tools that were already enhanced.

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: Celery task ID for the enhancement job

estimated_cost_cents.title: Estimated Cost Cents
estimated_cost_cents.description: Estimated cost in cents for the full enhancement run

estimated_duration_seconds.title: Estimated Duration Seconds
estimated_duration_seconds.description: Estimated duration in seconds for the full run

remaining_credits.title: Remaining Credits
remaining_credits.description: AI enhancement credits remaining. None = unlimited (Pro tier).

AIEnhancementResponse.title: AIEnhancementResponse
AIEnhancementResponse.description: Response returned immediately after submitting an enhancement job.

APIHealthResponse.title: APIHealthResponse
APIHealthResponse.description: Response from the API v1 health check.

scopes.title: Scopes
scopes.description: Permission scopes; see PRD § 13 for the full list.

ApiKeyCreateRequest.title: ApiKeyCreateRequest
ApiKeyCreateRequest.description: POST /api/v1/api-keys — create a new API key.

plaintext_key.title: Plaintext Key
plaintext_key.description: Shown ONCE at creation. Store securely.

ApiKeyCreatedResponse.title: ApiKeyCreatedResponse
ApiKeyCreatedResponse.description:
Response for POST /api/v1/api-keys — includes the plaintext ONCE.

The plaintext is shown in the UI one time and never again. After
creation, only `ApiKeyResponse` (no `plaintext_key`) is returned.

ApiKeyListResponse.title: ApiKeyListResponse
ApiKeyListResponse.description: Response for GET /api/v1/api-keys — list of keys.

ApiKeyResponse.title: ApiKeyResponse
ApiKeyResponse.description: Response for listing API keys. NEVER returns the plaintext.

CheckoutResponse.title: CheckoutResponse
CheckoutResponse.description: Response for a successful checkout session creation.

ConnectPanelResponse.title: ConnectPanelResponse
ConnectPanelResponse.description:
GET /api/v1/servers/{id}/connect — connection details for the gateway.

Returned when a user views the "Connect" panel for their deployed MCP
server.  Includes copy-ready config snippets for Claude Desktop and
Cursor.

CredentialListResponse.title: CredentialListResponse
CredentialListResponse.description: Response wrapper for listing all credentials on a server.

CredentialResponse.title: CredentialResponse
CredentialResponse.description: Response when listing credentials. NEVER returns the value.

CredentialTestResponse.title: CredentialTestResponse
CredentialTestResponse.description:
Response for a credential test request.

Note: The F1 spec names this field ``response_time_ms``; we use ``latency_ms``
for consistency with pre-existing code in the dashboard and analytics pipeline.

new_slug.title: New Slug
new_slug.description: Kebab-case slug (3-50 chars). Auto-generated from name if omitted.

Finding.title: Finding
Finding.description: A single security finding from the scanner.

ForgotPasswordRequest.title: ForgotPasswordRequest
ForgotPasswordRequest.description: Request body for initiating a password reset.

HealthResponse.title: HealthResponse
HealthResponse.description: Response from the root health check endpoint.

MCPServerCreate.title: MCPServerCreate
MCPServerCreate.description: Request body for creating a new MCP server.

MCPServerUpdate.title: MCPServerUpdate
MCPServerUpdate.description: Request body for updating an MCP server.

estimated_propagation_seconds.title: Estimated Propagation Seconds
estimated_propagation_seconds.description: Estimated time for the status change to propagate through the gateway

PlanInfo.title: PlanInfo
PlanInfo.description: A single plan as returned by GET /api/v1/billing/plans.

PortalRequest.title: PortalRequest
PortalRequest.description: POST /api/v1/billing/portal — get a Stripe customer portal link.

PortalResponse.title: PortalResponse
PortalResponse.description: Response for a successful portal session creation.

ResetPasswordRequest.title: ResetPasswordRequest
ResetPasswordRequest.description: Request body for completing a password reset.

ScanHistoryResponse.title: ScanHistoryResponse
ScanHistoryResponse.description: Paginated list of scan results for a server.

ScanTriggerResponse.title: ScanTriggerResponse
ScanTriggerResponse.description: Response when a security scan is initiated.

SecurityReport.title: SecurityReport
SecurityReport.description: JSON export of a server's security posture.

ServerVersionResponse.title: ServerVersionResponse
ServerVersionResponse.description: A version snapshot of a server's configuration.

SpecUploadResponse.title: SpecUploadResponse
SpecUploadResponse.description: Response for a successful spec upload/fetch.

TeamAcceptRequest.title: TeamAcceptRequest
TeamAcceptRequest.description: POST /api/v1/team/accept — accept an invitation by token.

TeamInvitationResponse.title: TeamInvitationResponse

TeamInviteRequest.title: TeamInviteRequest
TeamInviteRequest.description: POST /api/v1/team/invite — invite a user by email.

TeamResponse.title: TeamResponse
TeamResponse.description:
Team representation.

``member_count`` and ``current_user_role`` are populated by the
service layer — they are not stored directly on the Team model.

TestConnectionResponse.title: TestConnectionResponse
TestConnectionResponse.description:
POST /api/v1/servers/{id}/test-connection — result of a dry-run call.

Unlike the credential test (which tests auth), this tests the full
MCP tool invocation pipeline from gateway through upstream.

accepted_tools.title: Accepted Tools
accepted_tools.description: Tool names whose AI enhancements to accept

rejected_tools.title: Rejected Tools
rejected_tools.description: Tool names whose AI enhancements to reject (keep original)

custom_edits.title: Custom Edits
custom_edits.description: Manual overrides keyed by tool name, value is a dict of fields to set

selected_tool_names.title: Selected Tool Names
selected_tool_names.description: Names of tools the user selected to include

ToolUpdateRequest.title: ToolUpdateRequest
ToolUpdateRequest.description:
Request body for PATCH /api/v1/servers/{id}/tools/{tool_name}.

All fields are optional. Only non-None values are applied.
